query: Delhi Safdarjung maximum temperature forecast May 25-31 2026
answer:
The sub-agent has returned comprehensive multi-model forecast data. Here is my full synthesis:

---

# Delhi Safdarjung Maximum Temperature Forecast Research: May 25–31, 2026

## Current Conditions (May 22, 2026)
- **Observed temperature** (evening IST): 36°C with haze
- **Today's forecast maximum**: 42.0°C (Open-Meteo blended model)

## Multi-Model Forecast Consensus for May 25–31

Four numerical weather prediction models were queried at the Safdarjung coordinates (28.5844°N, 77.2088°E):

| Date | ECMWF (°C) | ICON (°C) | Best Match (°C) | GFS (°C) | **Consensus (excl. GFS)** |
|------|-----------|----------|-----------------|---------|--------------------------|
| May 25 | 42.8 | 41.9 | 42.8 | 46.7 | **42–43°C** |
| May 26 | 42.6 | 42.0 | 43.1 | 46.5 | **42–43°C** |
| May 27 | 42.6 | 42.7 | 43.2 | 46.8 | **42.5–43°C** |
| May 28 | 42.1 | 42.7 | 42.0 | 46.4 | **42–43°C** |
| May 29 | 38.7 | 38.7 | 38.5 | 46.3 | **38.5–39°C** (sharp drop) |
| May 30 | 34.6 | — | 34.8 | 43.3 | **~35°C** (significant cooling, rain expected) |
| May 31 | 37.2 | — | 37.3 | 40.1 | **~37°C** (partial recovery) |

**Key forecast signal**: Three out of four models agree on a pronounced temperature drop around **May 29–30**, with maximums falling from ~43°C to ~35°C, associated with ~1.8 mm precipitation (likely pre-monsoon thunderstorm or western disturbance). The GFS model is a known warm-biased outlier for Delhi, running **3–4°C hotter** than the others.

## Historical Context (May 25–31 at Safdarjung, 2015–2025)
- **11-year mean of period maximums**: 41.6°C
- **11-year median**: 42.5°C
- **Range**: 34.5°C (2023, cool monsoon year) to 46.0°C (2024, extreme heatwave)
- **Years reaching ≥45°C**: 1 out of 11 (2024 only)
- **2026 forecast (42–43°C) is slightly above median** — comparable to 2018 (43.0°C peak)

## Heatwave Assessment
- **IMD heatwave threshold for Delhi**: ≥45°C or ≥+4.5°C departure from normal (~40°C)
- **2026 forecast departure**: +2 to +3°C above normal → **below heatwave threshold** per ECMWF/ICON consensus
- The GFS outlier of 46–47°C would qualify as a heatwave/severe heatwave if verified
- Historical base rate of reaching 45°C during this window: ~10–18%

## Key Takeaways
- **May 25–28**: Sustained hot spell with maximums of **42–43°C** (consensus), above normal but below heatwave criteria
- **May 29–30**: Sharp cooling to **35–39°C** with precipitation
- **May 31**: Partial temperature recovery to **~37°C**
- GFS is a notable warm outlier; if verified, temperatures could reach 46–47°C (heatwave territory)
- This period is forecast to be moderately hot but not exceptional by Delhi late-May standards

---

key_facts: ECMWF/ICON/Best-Match consensus forecasts Delhi Safdarjung max temperatures of 42–43°C for May 25–28, 2026, which is 2–3°C above the May normal of ~40°C but below the IMD heatwave threshold of 45°C.
GFS model is a notable warm outlier, projecting 46–47°C for May 25–28, which would constitute an IMD heatwave if verified. GFS has a known warm bias for Delhi.
All models except GFS agree on a sharp temperature drop around May 29–30, with maximums falling to ~35°C, associated with ~1.8 mm precipitation (pre-monsoon thunderstorm or western disturbance).
May 31 shows partial temperature recovery to approximately 37°C.
Historical 11-year median (2015–2025) peak maximum for May 25–31 at Safdarjung is 42.5°C; the 2026 forecast of 42–43°C is near median, comparable to 2018.
The extreme 2024 heatwave saw 46.0°C during this same window — well above 2026 consensus forecasts.
Current observed temperature on May 22, 2026 evening is 36°C with haze; today's forecast maximum was 42.0°C.
Historical base rate of reaching ≥45°C during May 25–31 at Safdarjung is approximately 10–18% (1 out of 11 years in 2015–2025).
wttr.in/WorldWeatherOnline shows 46°C for May 25, aligning more with GFS than ECMWF/ICON consensus.
